_comment: Cheat-table library for GameNative IIC. Each table is keyed to a game by store source + id (same identity scheme as gamefixes/registry.json). On Box64 memory addresses are NOT stable across runs, so a cheat is a RECIPE that drives the in-process memory engine (scan -> narrow -> freeze), NOT a hardcoded address. The schema is forward-compatible with AOB pattern auto-resolution (the optional 'aob' block on a cheat) so one-tap cheats can be added later without a format change. Ship seed tables here; OTA-update by committing this file (no app release needed) — fetched from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mayusi/IsItCompatible-GameNative/main/cheattables/registry.json

tables.title: Hollow Knight
tables._comment: Seed table — recipe-based. Values are guided scans the user confirms once; addresses resolve at runtime so they survive Box64 relocation.

recipe.prompt: Open your inventory and read your current Geo amount, then enter it below.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Spend or collect some Geo so the number changes, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: guided_known: user enters the current value -> scanNew(i32). Then narrowPrompt + new value -> scanNext(EXACT). When narrowed to a few, freeze at freezeValue.

recipe.prompt: Read your current health (number of masks, usually 5-9). Enter it.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take damage so you lose a mask, then enter the new (lower) mask count.
recipe._comment: Small-range value -> may need 2-3 narrow passes; the UI loops scanNext until few matches remain.

recipe.prompt: Read your current gold (top-right of the screen) and enter it.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Buy or sell something so your gold changes, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: Stardew gold is a plain i32 in most builds.

recipe.prompt: Read your current HP (the number on the health bar) and enter it.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take a hit so HP drops, then enter the new HP value.
recipe._comment: Hades stores HP as a float (f32) — the UI encodes the entered value via floatToRawBits before scanning.

tables.title: Devil May Cry HD Collection
tables._comment: AppID 631510 covers the HD Collection (DMC1/2/3 SE). All cheats target DMC3 SE (dmc3.exe, x64). DATA TYPES: HP and DT are float32 (one gauge bar ~= 1000.0; full HP 3000.0-20000.0 with upgrades; DT ~3000.0 per bar). Red Orbs are uint32. APPROACH: the [RELIABLE] guided_known freeze cheats use the proven scan->freeze engine with NO dependence on Box64 dynarec SMC handling (the only thing that worked end-to-end on-device). The [EXPERIMENTAL] aob_patch cheats are true one-tap NOPs that may fail silently under Box64 dynarec if the JIT cached the pre-patch block — try them, but if HP/DT still drains use the freeze cheats. Pointer chain (for a future true-one-tap data freeze, NOT yet implemented): actorBase = *(dmc3.exe+0xC90DF8 -> +0x28); HP @ actorBase+0x411C, DT @ actorBase+0x3EB8 (source serpentiem/ddmk Mary/Memory.ixx + Mary/Training.ixx, fork berthrage/DMC3-Crimson). NOP sites verified from ddmk Training.ixx.

recipe.prompt: One-tap: keeps your Vitality at max. Works in-mission.
recipe._comment: VERIFIED ON-DEVICE (2026-06-15): in-game proxy dinput8.dll resolves actor = *(*(dmc3.exe+0xC90E28)+0x18), writes HP@+0x411C = maxHP@+0x40EC every 16ms. dmc3diag read HP=5300/6000, freeze held through enemy hits. writeMax keeps the bar full without over-range glitching.

recipe.prompt: One-tap: keeps your Devil Trigger gauge full (once you have DT runes).
recipe._comment: Same proven actor chain as HP: DT@+0x3EB8 = maxDT@+0x3EBC every tick. Only takes effect once you have at least one DT rune (maxDT>0); the engine skips the write when maxDT is 0 so it never forces a fake gauge.

recipe.prompt: Look at your Vitality gauge (the orange bar). One full bar = 1000. If you have 3 bars, enter 3000. For partial HP, estimate from the bars (e.g. 2.5 bars ~ 2500). Enter a whole number.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take a hit so your HP drops, then re-read the gauge and enter the new lower value. Repeat until narrowed to a few matches.
recipe._comment: RELIABLE — proven scan->freeze engine, architecture-agnostic, no dynarec dependency. HP is f32, one bar ~1000.0, max ~20000.0 fully upgraded. Freeze at 20000 locks near max. If freeze looks odd, lower to 3000 (1 bar).

recipe.prompt: Activate Devil Trigger briefly then cancel it and look at your DT gauge. Each DT orb/star ~ 1000. If you have 3 DT orbs, enter 3000. Enter the current DT as a whole number.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Let DT drain a little (activate DT for a second), then enter the new lower value.
recipe._comment: RELIABLE — proven scan->freeze, architecture-agnostic. DT is f32 at actorBase+0x3EB8. One DT star ~1000.0. Freeze 3000 = always-full 3-star DT; raise if more stars unlocked.

recipe.prompt: Look at your Red Orbs count in the top-left HUD. Enter the exact number shown.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Kill an enemy or break an object to collect some orbs so the number changes, then enter the new total.
recipe._comment: RELIABLE — Red Orbs = uint32 at MissionData+0x38 (per-mission dynamic alloc, no static chain under Box64 ASLR), so guided i32 scan. Narrows quickly. Applies across DMC1/2/3.

recipe.prompt: One-tap: NOPs the main HP-drain instruction so damage stops subtracting Vitality. EXPERIMENTAL under Box64 dynarec — if HP still drains, use the [RELIABLE] freeze cheat instead.
recipe._comment: EXPERIMENTAL aob_patch. Source ddmk Training.ixx ToggleInfiniteHitPoints site 1 (dmc3.exe+0x88517). Sig 'subss xmm0,xmm8' (5B) + 'movss [rax+08],xmm0' = unique 10B window. Patch 5x NOP at offset 0.

recipe.prompt: One-tap: NOPs the DT charge-drain so the gauge stops depleting when using Devil Trigger. EXPERIMENTAL under Box64 dynarec — if DT still drains, use the [RELIABLE] freeze cheat instead.
recipe._comment: EXPERIMENTAL. Source ddmk Training.ixx ToggleInfiniteMagicPoints site 2 (dmc3.exe+0x1E788A). 'subss xmm0,xmm1' (4B) + 'movss [rdi+0x3EB8],xmm0' — embedded 0x3EB8 (B8 3E 00 00) makes this 12B sig uniquely match once. Patch 4x NOP over the subss.

tables.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim Special Edition
tables._comment: Skyrim stores gold as an i32 counter; HP is f32. Gold is reliably scannable as a visible on-screen number. HP is displayed as a float (e.g. 115.0) in the console and stored as f32 internally.

recipe.prompt: Open your inventory and read your current gold amount (shown at bottom of inventory screen). Enter it exactly.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Buy or sell something to change your gold count, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: Skyrim gold is stored as a plain 32-bit integer. Confirmed by numerous Cheat Engine community tables.

recipe.prompt: Open the console (~ key) or look at your health bar. Your base health is shown on the stats page — enter your current HP as a whole number (e.g. 115 for 115 HP).
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take damage from an enemy so your HP drops, then enter the new lower HP value. Use whole numbers only.
recipe._comment: Skyrim HP is stored as f32. The scanner encodes the entered integer via floatToRawBits. Freeze at 500 (well above typical max for a new character, safe value).

tables.title: Terraria
tables._comment: Terraria currency exists in four tiers (Copper/Silver/Gold/Platinum) each stored separately as i32. Platinum coins (1 plat = 100 gold = 10,000 silver = 1,000,000 copper) are the most useful to scan — scan your current platinum count. Health is also i32 (displayed as a whole number).

recipe.prompt: Open your inventory and count your Platinum Coins (the gold-coloured large coins). Enter the exact number. If you have zero, earn one first by selling items.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Spend or pick up a platinum coin so the count changes, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: Platinum coin count is a separate i32 field. Scanning for 0 will have too many results — start with at least 1 platinum. Freezing at 99 gives 99 platinum coins (near infinite spending power).

recipe.prompt: Look at your HP display (top-left). Enter your current HP as shown (e.g. 200 for 200/400 HP).
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take a hit from an enemy so your HP drops, then enter the new value.
recipe._comment: Terraria HP is stored as an i32. Max base HP is 400, max boosted is 500 with accessories. Freezing at 500 is safe.

tables.title: Cuphead
tables._comment: Cuphead stores HP as a very small integer (typically 3 for normal, up to 6 with accessibility assist mode). Small values mean more narrow passes are needed — the scanner will loop until few candidates remain. Coins are also a small i32.

recipe.prompt: Note your HP hearts in the top-left (usually 3 hearts = 3 HP). Enter your current HP count as a number.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take a hit from an enemy or projectile so you lose one heart, then enter the new lower count. Expect to narrow 3-4 times since the value range is very small.
recipe._comment: HP is a small i32 (typically 1-6). Many candidates will remain after first scan; the UI must loop narrowing. Freezing at 3 keeps you at full standard health.

recipe.prompt: Visit the shop and note your coin count (shown at the bottom of the shop screen). Enter it.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Spend a coin on an item in the shop, then enter the new lower count.
recipe._comment: Coins are a plain i32. Scanning in the shop screen while the value is visible makes narrowing easiest.

tables.title: Dead Cells
tables._comment: Dead Cells uses Cells (the main upgrade currency) and Gold (per-run spending money), both stored as i32. Community Cheat Engine tables confirm 4-byte integer for both. Health is also i32.

recipe.prompt: Look at your gold counter (shown at top of HUD during a run). Enter the exact amount you currently have.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Pick up a gold pouch or spend gold at a shop so the value changes, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: Gold is per-run currency stored as i32. Narrows quickly since the initial value is fairly unique.

recipe.prompt: Kill a few enemies so you have some cells, then look at your Cells counter. Enter the current count.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Kill another enemy to pick up a cell, then enter the new higher count.
recipe._comment: Cells are the permanent upgrade currency (carried between runs to the Collector). Stored as i32. Collect at least 1 cell before scanning so you don't start from 0.

recipe.prompt: Look at your HP bar and the number shown above it. Enter your current HP value.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take a hit so HP drops, then enter the new value.
recipe._comment: Dead Cells HP is i32. Starting HP is 100 for most builds. Freezing at 100 keeps full HP.

tables.title: Celeste
tables._comment: Celeste does not have a traditional currency or HP bar — the player dies in one hit. The most useful scannable values are: Death Count (i32, visible on chapter select screen) and Strawberry Count (i32). Neither provides a gameplay advantage by itself, but strawberry count can be frozen to preserve a target for speedruns. Skipping this table: nothing here is reliably freezable for an 'infinite' gameplay advantage. No cheats authored — one-hit-death game, no currency, no HP. SKIP.

tables.title: Undertale
tables._comment: Undertale stores HP and Gold as plain integers visible on screen. Gold is shown in the ITEM menu; HP is on the battle HUD.

recipe.prompt: Open your ITEM menu (or check the pause screen) and read your current GOLD amount. Enter it.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Buy something from a shop or receive gold from a battle to change the amount, then enter the new value.
recipe._comment: Undertale gold maxes out at 9999 in most contexts. Stored as a plain i32.

recipe.prompt: During or after a battle, read your HP from the battle menu (e.g. 20/20). Enter your current HP as a number.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take damage in a battle so HP drops, then enter the new lower HP value.
recipe._comment: Undertale HP is an integer. Max base HP is 20 but can be raised. Freezing at 99 keeps you at very high HP.

tables.title: Vampire Survivors
tables._comment: Vampire Survivors stores Gold (permanent upgrade currency) and in-run gold as i32 values. HP is shown as a whole number on the HUD. Note: the game runs under Godot/Electron, so process memory is accessible. Community trainers confirm i32 for coins and HP.

recipe.prompt: After a run ends (or on the main menu), read your total Gold Coins shown on the PowerUp/shop screen. Enter the exact amount.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Spend coins on a PowerUp upgrade so the amount decreases, then enter the new lower total.
recipe._comment: Permanent gold (used between runs for PowerUps) is an i32. Best to scan in the PowerUp/main menu where the value is clearly shown.

recipe.prompt: During a run, look at your HP number displayed below your character or in the HUD. Enter the current HP value as shown (it may be a decimal like 120.5 — just enter the nearest whole number).
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take damage from an enemy so your HP drops, then enter the new value.
recipe._comment: Vampire Survivors HP is stored as f32 in most versions. Freezing at 500 keeps you well above death regardless of damage scaling.

tables.title: Slay the Spire
tables._comment: CAUTION: Slay the Spire runs on the JVM (Java Virtual Machine) which can relocate object heap memory mid-run. Guided scanning may work at a single save point but the address can become invalid after combat. Gold is 4-byte int; HP is 4-byte int. These cheats may need re-scanning each time you reload. Authored as best-effort — flag this to the user in the prompt.

recipe.prompt: Look at your gold total (shown in the top-right area of the map/shop screen). Enter the exact amount. NOTE: This game runs on the JVM and memory may shift after combat — you may need to re-scan after each major state change.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Visit a shop and buy something to change your gold, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: JVM heap can relocate. Works best when scanned immediately at a shop screen and frozen before entering combat. May become stale after combat ends.

recipe.prompt: Look at your HP (shown at top-left, e.g. 72/80). Enter your CURRENT HP as a whole number. NOTE: JVM memory may shift — re-scan if the cheat stops working after combat.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take a hit so HP drops, then enter the new current HP value.
recipe._comment: HP is 4-byte int in STS. Max HP is typically 80-100. Freezing at 999 keeps HP at a very high value that the game may cap to your max — still prevents death.

tables.title: The Binding of Isaac: Rebirth
tables._comment: Rebirth (and Afterbirth+, Repentance) stores HP as a small integer (number of red heart containers * 2 to account for half-hearts, so 6 = 3 full hearts). Coins are a plain i32 (max 99). Keys and bombs are also i32.

recipe.prompt: Look at your coin count in the HUD (bottom-left area, small coin icon). Enter the exact number shown.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Pick up a coin or spend a coin at a shop so the count changes, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: Coins are capped at 99 in-game. Stored as i32. Small range — may need 3+ narrowing passes.

recipe.prompt: Look at your key count (key icon in HUD). Enter the number of keys you currently have.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Use a key to open a door or chest, then enter the new lower key count.
recipe._comment: Keys stored as i32. Capped at 99. Small value range — use 3+ passes.

recipe.prompt: Look at your bomb count (bomb icon in HUD). Enter how many bombs you have.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Place a bomb so the count drops, then enter the new lower count.
recipe._comment: Bombs stored as i32. Capped at 99. Small value range — use 3+ passes.

tables.title: Cult of the Lamb
tables._comment: Cult of the Lamb stores Devotion (building resource) and Gold (coin currency) as integers. Fervor (the combat mana/spell gauge) is also integer-based. Community Cheat Engine tables confirm i32 for all these values.

recipe.prompt: Look at your Gold coin total (shown in the HUD during cult management or combat). Enter the exact amount.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Spend gold at a shop or gain gold from a run to change the amount, then enter the new value.
recipe._comment: Gold is i32. Works best scanning while in the cult base or shop where the value is stable.

recipe.prompt: Look at your Devotion count in the cult management screen. Enter the current amount shown.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Collect devotion from followers or spend some, then enter the new amount.
recipe._comment: Devotion is the main cult-building resource. Stored as i32.

recipe.prompt: During a dungeon run, look at your HP hearts in the top-left. Count your full hearts and enter the number (e.g. enter 3 for 3 hearts).
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take a hit from an enemy so you lose a heart, then enter the new lower heart count.
recipe._comment: Cult of the Lamb HP is stored as f32 in the Unity engine build. Typical max is 3-6 hearts. Freeze at 6 for max HP.

tables.title: Brotato
tables._comment: Brotato is a Godot engine game. HP (current and max), gold (in-wave pickup currency), and Materials (the between-wave upgrade currency) are stored as integers. Community Cheat Engine analysis confirms i32 for all these. Materials are most useful for buying upgrades between waves.

recipe.prompt: Between waves, look at your Materials count (the blue gem/crystal icon in the upgrade shop). Enter the exact amount shown.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Buy an upgrade in the shop to spend some materials, then enter the new lower amount.
recipe._comment: Materials are the permanent upgrade currency used between waves. Stored as i32 in Godot's memory layout.

recipe.prompt: During a wave, look at your current HP shown in the top-left corner. Enter the number exactly.
recipe.narrowPrompt: Take damage from an enemy so HP drops, then enter the new lower value.
recipe._comment: Brotato HP is i32. Starting HP depends on character (typically 100-150). Freezing at 150 is safe for all characters.
